name: Dave Bargeron
description: American trombonist and tuba player most famous for playing with the jazz-rock group Blood, Sweat, and Tears
abstract: David Wayne Bargeron (September 6, 1942 – January 18, 2025) was an American trombonist and tuba player who was a member of the jazz-rock group Blood, Sweat & Tears.

concerts.label: Clark Terry and His Big Band

works.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

concerts.label: An Evening with Blood, Sweat and Tears

works.t: You've Made Me So Very Happy

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet
Landmark Jazz Concerts: An Evening of Duke Ellington

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: on one session I played tuba along with Dave Bargeron, Don Butterfield and Tony Price.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: The other gig was the Red Garter and Dave Bargeron was working at the Red Garter, the trombone player and also a tuba player... And so I would come in and do the weekend, I met Howard... we would hang out
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Stewart interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Daley
about_quotes.evidence: Howard, you know, singlehandedly evolved the tuba, because then he passed it on to people like Bob Stewart, Dave Bargeron, Earl McIntyre, and a host of other people he passed this information on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joseph Daley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 18, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: So I went down there and I met all these people... Dave Bargeron... And so Howard Johnson and I hit it off... I started playing with Gil Evans for the last 15 years of Gil’s life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023
